name: chi (Lightweight Idiomatic Go HTTP Router)

auth.notes: HTTP router; integrate any auth middleware

performance.notes: Comparable to net/http; minimal routing overhead; faster than Gorilla Mux

agent_readiness.notes: Lightweight, idiomatic Go router compatible with net/http. chi.NewRouter(). r.Get('/users/{id}', handler). chi.URLParam(r, 'id'). Middleware stack: r.Use(middleware.Logger). Sub-routers: r.Route('/admin', func(r chi.Router) {}). Compatible with any net/http middleware. chi/middleware: Logger, Recoverer, RealIP, Compress, Timeout, Throttle. No reflection, no magic — just idiomatic Go. Used by government services (USA.gov), Cloudflare tools. Between net/http (bare) and Gin (feature-rich).

gotchas: Less opinionated than Gin — fewer built-ins
Routing tree memory overhead for very large route sets
chi.URLParam requires chi context (not usable outside chi)
best_when: Go APIs wanting idiomatic net/http compatibility
Projects needing composable middleware without framework magic
Teams preferring minimal dependencies over Gin's batteries
avoid_when: Need framework conveniences (JSON binding, validation) — use Gin or Echo
Need full framework with ORM, auth, etc.
summary: chi is a lightweight, idiomatic Go HTTP router fully compatible with net/http. r.Get('/users/{id}', handler). Sub-routers, middleware stack (Logger, Recoverer, Timeout, Throttle). No magic — plain Go. Used by USA.gov, Cloudflare. Middle ground between bare net/http and full frameworks like Gin. Best for Go teams wanting clean, composable routing without framework opinions.
